title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. atrata
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. atrata, also known as the atrous columbine, is a subspecies of the wild columbine plant (Aquilegia vulgaris) native to Europe. This plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. atrata include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
